critic_raw:
**Issue 1: Stratified random split on 36-month time-series manufacturing data.** Section 3 uses a stratified random 15% test set across all 36 months. Equipment failure prediction has strong temporal structure: equipment ages, operational patterns shift seasonally, and maintenance history accumulates. Random assignment of observation windows to train and test allows windows from month 30 to train the LSTM while windows from month 5 appear in the test set. The LSTM's sequential state depends on prior windows — if adjacent windows from the same equipment are split between train and test randomly, the sequential context used during training is corrupted.

**Issue 2: The baseline withholds operator logs and context features, confounding model architecture with information access.** Section 5 specifies the logistic regression baseline uses "aggregated sensor statistics (mean, std, min, max, trend) extracted from the same 100-step rolling window" without operator logs or context features (equipment age, days since last maintenance, production shift). The RNN receives all three modalities: sensor sequences, encoded operator logs, and context features. The stated justification — "this ensures fair comparison... the RNN has the additional capacity to model multi-modal sequences" — conflates multi-modal information access with architecture. A logistic regression trained on sensor statistics + context features + log summaries would isolate whether the LSTM's sequential modeling adds value over feature aggregation.

**Issue 3: Concatenating sensor sequences and log sequences as a single RNN input assumes temporal alignment that is not guaranteed.** Section 4 specifies: "Concatenate the 100-step sensor sequence, the 50-step encoded log sequence, and context features into a single multi-modal input to the RNN." Sensor readings are at 1-minute intervals (100 steps = ~1.67 hours); operator log entries are at irregular intervals (50 entries per window may span days or hours depending on activity). Concatenating sequences of different temporal granularities as if they are co-registered time steps is not valid — the LSTM will interpret log entry N as occurring simultaneously with sensor step N, which is false and introduces systematic misalignment.
all_issues_raised: Stratified random sampling across 36 months of time-series sensor data assigns future-month windows to training and past-month windows to test, and the LSTM's sequential state dependencies are corrupted when adjacent windows from the same equipment are randomly split across partitions.

The logistic regression baseline receives only aggregated sensor statistics while the RNN receives sensor sequences plus operator logs plus context features — the comparison confounds model architecture with information access, making it impossible to attribute performance differences to the LSTM architecture alone.

Sensor sequences (100 steps at 1-minute intervals) and operator log sequences (50 entries at irregular timestamps) are concatenated into a single multi-modal RNN input, treating temporal alignment between modalities as exact when operator logs occur at arbitrary intervals — this misalignment is systematic and will corrupt the LSTM's temporal state.
all_issues_adjudicated: Stratified random sampling across 36 months of time-series sensor data assigns future-month windows to training and past-month windows to test, and the LSTM's sequential state dependencies are corrupted when adjacent windows from the same equipment are randomly split across partitions.

The logistic regression baseline receives only aggregated sensor statistics while the RNN receives sensor sequences plus operator logs plus context features — the comparison confounds model architecture with information access, making it impossible to attribute performance differences to the LSTM architecture alone.

Sensor sequences (100 steps at 1-minute intervals) and operator log sequences (50 entries at irregular timestamps) are concatenated into a single multi-modal RNN input, treating temporal alignment between modalities as exact when operator logs occur at arbitrary intervals — this misalignment is systematic and will corrupt the LSTM's temporal state.
